name: render-automation
description: Automate Render tasks via Rube MCP (Composio): services, deployments, projects. Always search tools first for current schemas.
capabilities: List and filter Render services by name, type, or pagination
Trigger manual deployments for any Render service
Monitor deployment status and retrieve build logs
author: sickn33
securitySummary: This is a documentation-only skill containing no executable code. The static findings flagged markdown backticks and URL references as potential security issues, but these are false positives. The skill provides documentation for using Rube MCP tools to manage Render services - no shell execution, network requests, or cryptographic operations occur. All detected patterns are documentation artifacts.
valueStatement: This skill automates Render cloud platform operations including service management, deployment triggering, and status monitoring through the Rube MCP integration.
limitations: Requires Rube MCP server to be configured with Render toolkit
Cannot create new Render services - only manages existing ones
Deployment polling intervals should respect API rate limits
Requires active Render account with connected authentication

useCases.title: Deploy applications to Render
useCases.description: Trigger and monitor deployments for web services, static sites, workers, or cron jobs from Claude or Codex

useCases.title: Monitor deployment status
useCases.description: Check deployment progress, retrieve build logs, and verify successful deployments

useCases.title: Manage Render infrastructure
useCases.description: List services, filter by type, and organize projects across Render account

promptTemplates.title: List all web services
promptTemplates.prompt: List all web services on my Render account using the render-automation skill.

promptTemplates.title: Deploy a specific service
promptTemplates.prompt: Trigger a deployment for my service called 'my-app' and monitor until it completes.

promptTemplates.title: Check deployment status
promptTemplates.prompt: What's the current status of deployment dep-xxxx for service srv-xxxx?

promptTemplates.title: List all projects
promptTemplates.prompt: Show me all Render projects and their associated services.

bestPractices: Always call RUBE_SEARCH_TOOLS first to get current tool schemas before use
Use pagination cursors when listing services to avoid missing results
Poll deployment status at 10-30 second intervals to respect rate limits
antiPatterns: Do not trigger multiple deployments simultaneously without checking queue status
Do not assume deployment succeeded without checking status for 'live'
Do not skip service ID resolution - always use LIST_SERVICES to get correct IDs

faq.question: What is Rube MCP?
faq.answer: Rube MCP is a Model Context Protocol server by Composio that provides tools for interacting with cloud services like Render. It must be configured separately from this skill.

faq.question: Do I need a Render account?
faq.answer: Yes, you need an active Render account. The skill requires authentication through Rube MCP which connects to your Render account.

faq.question: Can this skill create new services?
faq.answer: No, this skill only manages existing services. It can list, deploy, and monitor services but cannot create new ones.

faq.question: How do I find my service ID?
faq.answer: Use RENDER_LIST_SERVICES with the name filter to find your service. The response includes the service ID in format 'srv-xxxxxxxx'.

faq.question: What deployment statuses should I watch for?
faq.answer: 'live' means success. 'build_failed' or 'update_failed' indicate errors. Other statuses like 'build_in_progress' are intermediate states.

faq.question: What rate limits apply?
faq.answer: Render API has rate limits. Poll at 10-30 second intervals and avoid bulk operations in quick succession.
